response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{1, 12, -17, -3, -11, -5\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{1 + 12 + (-17) + (-3) + (-11) + (-5)}{6}
$$

$$
= \frac{1 + 12 - 17 - 3 - 11 - 5}{6}
= \frac{-19}{6}
\approx -3.1667
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

$$
\begin{align*}
(1 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (4.1667)^2 \approx 17.3611 \\
(12 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (15.1667)^2 \approx 229.89 \\
(-17 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (-13.8333)^2 \approx 191.36 \\
(-3 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (0.1667)^2 \approx 0.0278 \\
(-11 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (-7.8333)^2 \approx 61.36 \\
(-5 - (-3.1667))^2 &= (-1.8333)^2 \approx 3.3611 \\
\end{align*}
$$

---

### Step 3: Sum the squared differences

$$
17.3611 + 229.89 + 191.36 + 0.0278 + 61.36 + 3.3611 \approx 492.39
$$

---

### Step 4: Divide by $n - 1 = 5$ (sample variance)

$$
\text{Sample Variance} = \frac{492.39}{5} \approx 98.48
$$

---

### Step 5: Take the square root to get the sample standard
